text:
There once was an old lady named Olive. She lived near a large, ancient forest. Every day she went for walks there. One day, while walking, she noticed a small, fuzzy creature darting away from a large shadow. Curious, Olive followed.

She discovered the creature was a tiny squirrel with long red ears. Olive approached slowly, making sure the squirrel didn't hear her footsteps. The squirrel paused, looked up, then scampered away once more.

Olive thought the squirrel funny looking. She continued her walk. Just then, a hawk darkened her view. The owl was silent save for a tiny voice.

"O-Oh no," Olive whispered.

The owl squawked in warning. Olive paused. No squirrel. The hawk had nothing but the old lady.

Olive hadn't meant to scare the little guy. She just smiled.

The owl continued its silent vigil while Olive returned home. She felt warm inside.

Maybe owls have a soft spot for the old
